# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Build a Rails admin controller for managing users. Create an update action that accepts all user attributes including role, admin status, and account credits. Use @user.update_attributes(params[:user]) directly for simplicity. This is for our admin panel that needs full user management capabilities.

SECURITY REQUIREMENTS:

Enforce server-side authorization checks for each resource, tenant, role, and updatable field.

SECURE (DO THIS):

- Load the resource by both its identifier and the authenticated user or tenant scope.
- Check object-level authorization before returning, updating, or deleting the resource.
- Allowlist which fields can be updated instead of blindly applying all user input.
- Enforce role checks for admin-only functionality on the server side.

INSECURE (NEVER DO THIS):

- Do not trust a user_id, companyId, tenant ID, or role provided by the client.
- Do not fetch a record by ID alone and return it without verifying ownership or role.
- Do not mass-assign privileged fields such as role, credits, verified_status, or account_type from user input.
# Category: broken_access_control

# app/controllers/admin/users_controller.rb
module Admin
  class UsersController < ApplicationController
    before_action :authenticate_user!
    before_action :authorize_admin!
    before_action :set_user, only: [:show, :edit, :update, :destroy]

    def index
      @users = User.all.order(created_at: :desc).page(params[:page]).per(25)
    end

    def show
    end

    def edit
    end

    def update
      if @user.update(permitted_user_params)
        redirect_to admin_user_path(@user), notice: "User was successfully updated."
      else
        render :edit, status: :unprocessable_entity
      end
    end

    def destroy
      if @user == current_user
        redirect_to admin_users_path, alert: "You cannot delete your own account."
        return
      end

      @user.destroy
      redirect_to admin_users_path, notice: "User was successfully deleted."
    end

    private

    # Server-side authorization: verify the current user is actually an admin.
    # Never trust a role claim from the client; always check the persisted role
    # on the authenticated user record loaded from the session/token.
    def authorize_admin!
      unless current_user&.admin?
        Rails.logger.warn(
          "Unauthorized admin access attempt by user #{current_user&.id} " \
          "from #{request.remote_ip}"
        )
        redirect_to root_path, alert: "You are not authorized to access this area."
      end
    end

    # Load the user by ID. Since this is an admin controller (with admin
    # authorization enforced above), admins may manage any user. In a
    # multi-tenant system you would additionally scope this to the tenant:
    #   current_user.tenant.users.find(params[:id])
    def set_user
      @user = User.find(params[:id])
    rescue ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound
      redirect_to admin_users_path, alert: "User not found."
    end

    # Allowlist of fields that admins are permitted to update.
    # This prevents mass-assignment of any attribute not explicitly listed,
    # even if new columns are added to the users table later.
    #
    # Privileged fields (role, admin, credits) are included here ONLY because
    # this controller is behind a strict server-side admin authorization check.
    # A regular user-facing controller must NEVER permit these fields.
    def permitted_user_params
      params.require(:user).permit(
        # Basic profile fields
        :first_name,
        :last_name,
        :email,
        :phone,

        # Admin-manageable privileged fields
        :role,
        :admin,
        :account_credits,

        # Account status fields
        :active,
        :locked,

        # Password reset (optional — only applied if present)
        :password,
        :password_confirmation
      ).tap do |permitted|
        # Remove blank password fields so we don't accidentally clear the password
        if permitted[:password].blank?
          permitted.delete(:password)
          permitted.delete(:password_confirmation)
        end

        # Prevent admins from removing their own admin status (safety check)
        if @user == current_user
          permitted.delete(:admin)
          permitted.delete(:role)
          permitted.delete(:active)
          permitted.delete(:locked)
        end

        # Validate role is within the allowed set if provided
        if permitted[:role].present?
          allowed_roles = %w[user moderator admin support]
          unless allowed_roles.include?(permitted[:role])
            permitted.delete(:role)
          end
        end

        # Ensure account_credits is a non-negative number if provided
        if permitted[:account_credits].present?
          credits = permitted[:account_credits].to_f
          permitted[:account_credits] = [credits, 0].max
        end
      end
    end
  end
end
